Home/Philosophy of Science

Branch Guide

Philosophy of Science

Method, explanation, induction, causation, and the standards that separate disciplined inquiry from pseudo-explanation.

  • Branch Philosophy of Science
  • Page form Branch guide
  • Best for understanding method, error-correction, and the limits of science
  • Difficulty Foundational to intermediate
  • Last revised May 22, 2026

Why This Branch Matters

the core distinctions, tensions, and future paths that make the topic worth reconstructing.

This topic matters because it organizes a cluster of questions that become harder to handle when they are compressed into a slogan.

A good route is to identify the strongest version of the idea, then test where it needs qualification, evidence, or a neighboring concept.

Core Tensions

The branch becomes useful when these pressures stay visible.

  • The central distinction
  • The strongest charitable version
  • The main pressure point
  • The neighboring question

Entry Points

Representative pages in this branch

  • Correlation and Causation A page on Correlation and Causation, written to clarify its role inside the Philosophy of Science branch.
